business.headline: Why your car insurance may not pay when you need it mostAn accident, a flooded road, a damaged engine, and a car insurance claim is filed with the expectation that the insurer will step in. But for many, that moment turns into something else entirely: confusion, partial payouts, or in some cases, outright rejection.

India's digital payments boom has a growing fraud problemIndia's digital boom has made payments effortless, but also easier to exploit. From fake identities to instant scams, fraudsters are moving faster than the systems meant to stop them, and users are paying the price.

Govt approves 2% dearness allowance hike for central govt employees, pensionersThe Union Cabinet approved a 2% hike in Dearness Allowance for government employees and pensioners, along with key decisions including a Rs 13,000 crore Sovereign Maritime Fund and extension of PMGSY till 2028.

On paper, compliant: How labour laws are quietly underminedIn the third episode in this series on labour laws and Inspector Raj, this article examines how employers maintain compliance on paper while systematically evading legal obligations in practice.

Digital gold, ETFs or SGBs: Where should you invest this Akshaya Tritiya?Akshaya Tritiya is here, and gold buying is once again in focus. But instead of just buying jewellery, many are now exploring smarter ways to invest. So, which option, digital gold, ETFs or SGBs, makes the most sense?
Oil prices slide over 10% as Iran opens HormuzIran's foreign minister said all commercial ships can pass through the Strait of Hormuz during the remaining ceasefire period.

Your FY27 game plan: Simplifying insurance, investments, tax and emergency fundPart 3 of our 'MONEY MAKEOVER' series explains why the start of the financial year is the right time to focus on insurance, investments, tax and emergency fund planning to guide the rest of the year.

Explained: Why India slipped to 6th-largest economy despite strong growthIndia has slipped to the 6th-largest economy in the IMF's latest outlook. Why did it happen despite strong growth? IndiaToday.in spoke to economists to find out.
Sensex ends 500 points higher as HUL leads market rally with 5% riseThe S&P BSE Sensex added 504.86 points to end at 78,493.54, while the NSE Nifty50 gained 156.80 points to close at 24,353.55.

Sebi allows 50% IPO size cut without refiling: What it means for investorsThe regulator has taken this step as market participants are finding it difficult to mobilise resources and access capital markets in the current geopolitical environment.

Akshay Tritiya property plans? Don't miss these 5 key checksAkshay Tritiya is seen as the perfect day to make big financial moves, and property is high on many buyers' lists, but before you take the plunge, are you checking the right things?
DA hike delayed: Union body urges Govt to act quicklyThe timely announcement of DA will help restore confidence and ease concerns among lakhs of central government employees and pensioners.

Netflix shares slide over 9% as co-founder Reed Hastings steps downHastings has been a key figure in shaping Netflix into what it is today. He co-founded the company and played a major role in transforming it from a DVD-by-mail service into a global streaming giant.

Forced to mis-sell: IIIT graduate quits Rs 17 LPA bank job, cites sales pressureChirag Madaan's resignation from his Rs 17 lakh per annum banking job has reignited focus on mis-selling at financial institutions, an issue IndiaToday.in had earlier highlighted through its investigation into how sales targets and incentives shape what customers are sold.

Gautam Adani overtakes Mukesh Ambani to become Asia's richestThe latest ranking places Gautam Adani at the top spot in Asia, with Mukesh Ambani now in second place. Both remain among the top 25 richest people globally, even as their wealth levels have seen changes this year.

2% to 4% DA hike likely: Here's how much your monthly pay may riseA fresh DA revision is around the corner, and early estimates suggest a 2% to 4% hike, but how much extra could this actually add to your monthly salary? Let's have a look.

India slips to 6th-largest economy: What IMF's latest data showsIndia has slipped out of the world's top five economies in nominal GDP terms, going by the IMF's latest World Economic Outlook estimates. The change says more about currency than growth, with projections suggesting the loss in ranking may not last long.

Markets open flat as geopolitical tensions ease, but caution remainsThe BSE Sensex was trading at 78,030.99, up 42.31 points or 0.05% at around 9:43 am. The Nifty 50, however, slipped marginally by 6.85 points or 0.03% to 24,189.90.

Gurugram circle rates increase by up to 75%: How much will home prices rise?The revision in Gurugram circle rates comes at a time when the city continues to see strong demand due to its proximity to the airport and the presence of major offices and industries.

Rs 8 lakh crore stuck: How delayed payments are hurting India's MSMEsAcross India, the total value of such pending payments to micro, small and medium enterprises is estimated at Rs 8.1 lakh crore, according to industry estimates and government assessments.

entertainment.headline: Pawan Kalyan undergoes surgery: Ram Charan, Allu Arjun, celebs wish speedy recovery Pawan Kalyan underwent surgery after a sudden health issue and has been advised about 10 days of rest. His wife Anna Lezhneva confirmed he is recovering well, while several actors wished him a speedy recovery.

Salman Khan roasts Varun Dhawan in fun BTS video from Hai Jawani song. Watch A fun BTS video from Hai Jawani Toh Ishq Hona Hai, featuring Varun Dhawan and Salman Khan, has gone viral for their easy camaraderie. The track Vyah Karwado Ji, sung by Mika Singh and Asees Kaur, is set to be a lively addition to the film, which releases on May 22.

Akshay Kumar on Wamiqa being compared to Aishwarya, Katrina: She has her identity Akshay Kumar reacted to social media users comparing his Bhooth Bangla co-star Wamiqa Gavbi with Aishwarya Rai and Katrina Kaif.

Actor Shivaleeka Oberoi, filmmaker Abhishek Pathak welcome baby girl: Our precious Actor Shivaleeka Oberoi and filmmaker Abhishek Pathak have become parents to a baby girl. The couple shared the news with a heartfelt social media post.

Zanai remembers grandmother Asha Bhosle with rare pics: You are missed every second Zanai Bhosle shared an emotional post remembering her grandmother Asha Bhosle, who died on April 12 due to multiple organ failure.

Deepika Padukone, Ranveer Singh confirm second pregnancy with a cute pic Deepika Padukone and Ranveer Singh have announced that they are expecting their second child with a heartfelt Instagram post featuring their daughter Dua.

Why did India and the world watch Dhurandhar 2 despite propaganda claims? A four-hour cinematic experience that dares to unsettle got audiences to sit inside theatres and enjoy cinema as a community viewing experience - not just in India, but worldwide. This is the era of Dhurandhar: The Revenge that continues even 30 days into its box office run. But why? Why couldn't you ignore this Aditya Dhar juggernaut?

Bhooth Bangla box office Day 2: Film crosses Rs 60 crore globally on 1st Saturday Akshay Kumar's Bhooth Bangla has recorded a strong second-day performance, collecting Rs 60.50 crore worldwide and showing over 55 per cent growth from Day 1.

Dhurandhar 2 box office Day 31: Rs 60 crore to beat Baahubali 2, film holds steady Dhurandhar 2: The Revenge continues to edge closer to a major box office milestone, with its global total now only around Rs 60 crore short of Baahubali 2's 1,810.60 crore lifetime haul.

Ms Marvel's Mohan Kapur talks Hollywood, Bollywood and returning to theatre Actors Mohan Kapur and Vidya Malvade are returning to theatre with their new play Merry Go Round, set to be staged in Mumbai.

Dhurandhar actor Gaurav Gera on why he won't go back to being deeply in love again Actor Gaurav Gera admits he was deeply in love once, revealing the reason he would want to avoid going back to such a situation once again.

14-hour shifts, no lunch breaks: Archana Puran Singh slams film set work culture Archana Puran Singh raised concerns about long working hours and lack of basic facilities on film sets, calling out the impact on crew members. She highlighted how extended shifts and skipped meal breaks reflect a deeper issue in the industry's work culture.

Samay Raina says his grandfather was on a kill list, recalls Kashmiri Pandit exodus Samay Raina said his grandfather was on a hit list during the Kashmiri Pandit exodus and revealed how Kashmiri Muslims helped his family escape.

Was starting to get that image: Ali Asgar on quitting Dadi act in Kapil Sharma show Ali Asgar said he quit playing Dadi on The Kapil Sharma Show as he was starting to get offers only to play female roles. He added that he wanted to move beyond typecasting and grow as an actor.

Prakash Raj slams Pawan Kalyan over Women's Bill comment, challenges him to debate Prakash Raj has criticised actor and Andhra Pradesh Deputy Chief Minister Pawan Kalyan for his remarks on the Women's Reservation Bill. Prakash Raj accused Pawan Kalyan of misleading citizens and challenged him to a public debate.

Sonali Raut claims she got scabies on Bigg Boss Marathi 6: Rats, cockroaches on show Sonali Raut says she faced hygiene issues during her stint on Bigg Boss Marathi 6. Claiming that she developed scabies, and was exposed to rats and cockroaches on the show, she has raised the issue on social media and sent a legal notice to the makers.

Akshay Kumar says son Aarav earns Rs 4,500: He is learning fashion in villages Akshay Kumar says his son Aarav Bhatia has no plans to join films. He adds that Aarav has a Rs-4,500 job and is learning fashion from the grassroots level in villages.

Vijay Varma rejects rush culture, calls himself a work in progress Vijay Varma spoke about his journey in films, saying his growth has been steady. He also reflected on his success on streaming platforms and said he prefers to enjoy the present rather than chase milestones.

Par excellence: Aditya Dhar praises Dhurandhar choreographer Vijay Ganguly Director Aditya Dhar has praised Vijay Ganguly for his choreography in the Dhurandhar films. Dhar also credited Ganguly for his dance direction in Shararat, one of the viral songs this season.

general news.headline: 2 Americans detained at Srinagar airport after satellite phone found in luggageTwo US nationals were questioned and handed over to the police after a satellite phone was recovered from their bags during a security screening at Srinagar airport.

If you have physical needs...: Woman details months of harassment at TCS NashikIn her police complaint accessed by India Today TV, a woman employee has alleged months of harassment, stalking and religious slurs by five colleagues at Tata Consultancy Services' (TCS) Nashik BPO unit.

You gave me clearance to go, now firing: Indian tanker's frantic call in HormuzA frantic audio of an Indian-flagged tanker's radio message with Iran after being cleared to transit and then allegedly fired upon by Iranian forces in the Strait of Hormuz has emerged, forcing ships to turn back.

UP man kills twin daughters by slitting their throats, then calls police himselfThe police received information about the killings during the early hours of Sunday. Following the double-murder, the wife of the accused levelled serious allegations of torture against him, saying that he was an alcoholic and would regularly beat her.

US-Iran talks uncertain but Pak bans public transport in twin citiesPakistan's twin cities, Islamabad and Rawalpindi, have imposed sweeping curbs on public and goods transport and shut major hubs as they hope to hold the second round of US-Iran talks. The preparations come even as it isn't certain if the talks will take place at all.

Deepika Padukone, Ranveer Singh confirm second pregnancy with a cute picDeepika Padukone and Ranveer Singh have announced that they are expecting their second child with a heartfelt Instagram post featuring their daughter Dua.

Who is Trump to deny us our nuclear rights? Iran President draws red linePezeshkian expressed defiance as the tenuous ceasefire between the US and Iran is set to expire on April 22. The two sides have yet to come to an agreement on Iran's nuclear programme and control of the Strait of Hormuz.

Distress in India's Ceramic City: Cold kilns in factories, cold stoves at homesThe war in the Middle East has brought manufacturing in India's ceramic hub Khurja to a standstill, with 90% of the 300 units shutting shops temporarily. The election in West Bengal has added to the crisis. With workers scrambling to survive, Khurja is a microcosm of the distress in India's manufacturing hubs.

Why did India and the world watch Dhurandhar 2 despite propaganda claims?A four-hour cinematic experience that dares to unsettle got audiences to sit inside theatres and enjoy cinema as a community viewing experience - not just in India, but worldwide. This is the era of Dhurandhar: The Revenge that continues even 30 days into its box office run. But why? Why couldn't you ignore this Aditya Dhar juggernaut?

7 weeks after assassination, Iran yet to bury Ali Khamenei. Here's whyIran has yet to finalise the burial of Supreme Leader Ali Khamenei weeks after his assassination, with reports pointing to security concerns, war conditions and fears of unrest delaying a state funeral.

Ceding ground virtually? Census portal uses Chinese name for Arunachal town, slammedA retired IAF officer flagged that the Census self-enumeration portal wrongly showed Arunachal Pradesh's Pasighat town as Medog, which is a Chinese town across the LAC. The government said it was a technical mapping error, and had been promptly resolved.

Live: Tehran turns back 2 tankers in Hormuz, cites 'unauthorised transit'US-Israel-Iran War Live Updates: Fresh uncertainty grips the region as Iran says nuclear talks with the US have made progress but key gaps remain, even as Donald Trump claims "very good conversations" while warning against "blackmail" over the Strait of Hormuz. Just days before a fragile ceasefire deadline, Tehran has again closed the vital oil route after briefly reopening it, escalating tensions in the eighth week of the war that has killed thousands and disrupted global energy flows. Reports of gunfire on vessels, India flagging attacks on its ships, and threats of renewed US strikes underscore the volatility, with no date set for the next round of negotiations and the risk of further escalation looming. Follow indiatoday.in for all live updates.

Is that ship flying? The ghost, the myth and the physics of the Flying DutchmanVideos of ships appearing to hover above the sea have gone viral, but the explanation is pure science, a rare phenomenon called Fata Morgana. Here is how physics playing tricks with light is the answer to the Flying Dutchman legend.

Far from final deal: Iran flags gaps in talks with US as ceasefire deadline nearsIn a televised address, Iranian Parliament Speaker Mohammad Bagher Ghalibaf said that while some headway has been made in negotiations, major differences remain unresolved, signalling that talks are still at a preliminary stage.

Doctor by day, marathoner by dawn: Kartik Karkera chases Asian Games goldKartik Karkera has emerged as a wildcard in the Indian marathon scene. The Orthopedic doctor has won the Mumbai and Delhi marathons, at a pace that makes him one of India's fastest marathoners of all time. Currently in Nashik, Kartik speaks to India Today about his dream of winning Asian Games gold and representing India at the Olympics.

Surrender or you won't be spared: PM Modi's last warning to TMC goons, syndicatesPrime Minister Narendra Modi warned Trinamool Congress "goons and syndicates" to surrender by April 29, while promising welfare schemes for women ahead of the West Bengal polls.

From London, NYC pubs to Delhi mixers: 'Pitch Your Friend' is dating's new exportHandcrafted ByOur Editors The dating scene in India is now being powered by your wingperson in association with their pitching skills. Quite literally.
Question Time: Weekly News QuizThink you read the headlines this week? Let's test that!

Fast, small, deadly: Iran's mosquito fleet puts US Navy on edge in HormuzAmid US pressure in the Strait of Hormuz, Iran is leaning on its IRGC Navy's fast "mosquito boat" fleet and drone-missile tactics to disrupt shipping through asymmetric warfare, despite heavy losses to its larger warships.

Worth Rs 100 crore, founder still travels by auto in Mumbai. Here's whyVinod Chendhil, founder of Naturally Yours, took to X to describe the habits of a close friend who, despite significant financial success, has chosen not to upgrade his lifestyle in the way many would expect.

Akshay Kumar on Wamiqa being compared to Aishwarya, Katrina: She has her identityAkshay Kumar reacted to social media users comparing his Bhooth Bangla co-star Wamiqa Gavbi with Aishwarya Rai and Katrina Kaif.

Trump's 'favourite field marshal' Asim Munir's Iran ties a red flag for USGeneral Munir is playing a key backchannel mediator between Washington and Tehran. However, critics argue that this positioning is complicated by his reported long-standing proximity to senior figures within Iran's Islamic Revolutionary Guard Corps (IRGC).

Iran's representative downplays Hormuz shooting, says ties with India very strongIran's representative in India downplayed concerns over the reported shooting at two Indian-flagged ships in the Strait of Hormuz, stressing strong bilateral ties and calling for peace. India flagged "deep concern" and summoned the Iranian envoy after the vessels were fired upon by IRGC forces.

Dhurandhar 2 box office Day 31: Rs 60 crore to beat Baahubali 2, film holds steadyDhurandhar 2: The Revenge continues to edge closer to a major box office milestone, with its global total now only around Rs 60 crore short of Baahubali 2's 1,810.60 crore lifetime haul.

sports.headline: Hate hearing the word impossible: Shreyas Iyer's mantra for PBKS in IPL 2026 Shreyas Iyer's 'impossible' mantra is powering Punjab Kings' unbeaten IPL 2026 run as they gear up to face LSG, with the skipper stressing belief, clarity and finishing games.

Samson to replace Ruturaj as CSK's captain? R Ashwin makes bold prediction IPL 2026: Chennai Super Kings' poor run under Ruturaj Gaikwad has reignited leadership debates, with Ravichandran Ashwin suggesting Sanju Samson could take over captaincy in the near future. With ongoing struggles in big chases and injuries in the squad, CSK will rely on Samson's form and leadership potential moving forward.

KKR vs RR live: Varun's sensational spell hands Kolkata advantage Kolkata (KKR) vs Rajasthan (RR) live score, IPL 2026 today match: RR (117/4 in 15 ovs). Varun Chakravarthy's sensational spell of 4-0-14-3 has handed KKR the advantage in Kolkata. Varun has run through the RR top order and suddenly the game has been turned on its head.

PSL never offered contract: Blessing Muzarabani's agent hits out at 2-year ban Blessing Muzarabani's agency has hit out at PSL's two-year ban, claiming no contract was ever signed before his move to KKR, and urging the league to withdraw the decision.

IPL: KKR receive big boost as Matheesha Pathirana joins camp after injury layoff Sri Lankan pacer Matheesha Pathirana has finally arrived in the city to join Kolkata Knight Riders, giving a much-needed boost to the side that is currently languishing at the bottom of the IPL table.

IPL 2026: CSK coach confirms Ayush Mhatre's injury, talks about MS Dhoni's return IPL 2026: Chennai Super Kings batting coach Michael Hussey confirmed that young batter Ayush Mhatre suffered a hamstring tear during the match against Sunrisers Hyderabad, with scans pending to determine the severity of the injury.

Lalit Modi shuts down Pietersen's 'UK IPL' claim on Hundred: Not in this lifetime IPL founder Lalit Modi has firmly rejected comparisons between the Indian Premier League and The Hundred, issuing a sharp rebuttal to Kevin Pietersen's claim that England's franchise tournament could eventually mirror the IPL.

Unbeaten Punjab Kings aim to continue run against struggling Lucknow PBKS vs LSG Preview: Punjab Kings' resurgence under Ricky Ponting faces a stern test against an inconsistent Lucknow Super Giants, with Rishabh Pant under pressure to deliver.

Sangakkara urges Suryavanshi to keep being fearless, bat with sense of wonder IPL 2026: Kumar Sangakkara has urged Vaibhav Sooryavanshi to commit to his batting style, while staying grounde. He wants the teenage batter to continue enjoying his cricket despite his explosive IPL start.

Doctor by day, marathoner by dawn: Kartik Karkera chases Asian Games gold Kartik Karkera has emerged as a wildcard in the Indian marathon scene. The Orthopedic doctor has won the Mumbai and Delhi marathons, at a pace that makes him one of India's fastest marathoners of all time. Currently in Nashik, Kartik speaks to India Today about his dream of winning Asian Games gold and representing India at the Olympics.

BCCI to extend Ajit Agarkar's tenure as Chief Selector with 2027 World Cup in focus Ajit Agarkar is set for an extension as chief selector, with BCCI backing his tenure after India's ICC success, despite Test setbacks, ahead of the 2027 ODI World Cup.

Heinrich Klaasen unbothered by strike-rate critics after crucial win vs CSKIPL 2026: Heinrich Klaasen hit a fifty as Sunrisers Hyderabad beat Chennai Super Kings by 10 runs. He said he's unfazed by strike-rate criticism, with 283 runs in six games, Klaasen emphasised his role in anchoring innings for SRH.

Bizarre: Tractor rampage halts cricket match, pitch destroyed in shocking scenesA cricket tournament final in Jalgaon was abandoned after a man drove a tractor onto the field and destroyed the pitch mid-match, reportedly over a dispute linked to a local figure not being invited.

WrestleMania 42 Night 1: Rhodes retains title, Paige returns, Gunther prevailsCody Rhodes retained his title as WrestleMania 42 night one delivered chaos, surprise returns and multiple title changes, with Liv Morgan and Becky Lynch among the big winners.

Man Utd captain Bruno Fernandes in a rush to seal Champions League qualificationManchester United boosted their Champions League hopes with a 1–0 win over Chelsea FC, extending the hosts' losing streak to four matches. Captain Bruno Fernandes starred with an assist as Chelsea's top-five ambitions continued to fade amid a prolonged goal drought.

One dead, six injured in Nuerburgring multi-car crash featuring Max VerstappenA 66 year old driver died and six were injured after a multi car crash halted the Nuerburgring qualifiers, with Max Verstappen expressing shock and calling it a reminder of the dangers in motorsport.

KKR vs RR preview: Already in ruins, Eden braces for Vaibhav Sooryavanshi stormIPL 2026, KKR vs RR preview: The only winless team in the tournament, Kolkata Knight Riders brace for another stiff test as they welcome a wounded Rajasthan Royals side. Vaibhav Sooryavanshi will be in focus at Eden Gardens on Sunday, with the teenager keen to bounce back from a rare failure.

City vs Arsenal preview: Guardiola's final chance to win Premier LeagueManchester City and Arsenal meet in what could be the defining game of the Premier League title race, with Pep Guardiola calling it a "final" for City. Arsenal still lead the table, but with City holding a game in hand, Sunday's clash at the Etihad could completely shift the balance of the season.

IPL 2026 Play Of The Day: Resolute Hyderabad bowlers defy odds to choke ChennaiSRH vs CSK, IPL 2026: If someone had told you that a team would defend a sub-200 total in Hyderabad, you would have probably laughed it off and credited them with a fine sense of humour. After all, this is a venue where bowlers often come just to survive the carnage, not control it.

Made Ayush Mhatre run when limping! Ashwin slams CSK's poor injury managementIPL 2026, SRH vs CSK: Former spinner R Ashwin did not hold back in his critique of Chennai Super Kings, saying he was shocked to see the five-time champions let Ayush Mhatre carry on despite a hamstring injury on Saturday. Ashwin added that those in the commentary box could not believe their eyes, slamming CSK's poor injury management.

What's wrong with Head? Pundits analyse SRH batter's lukewarm start to IPLAaron Finch and Ambati Rayudu dissected the reason behind Travis Head's lacklustre start to the IPL 2026 campaign. Head has scored just 143 runs in six matches.

Yellow mellowed at Uppal as disciplined SRH snatch win from CSK in HyderabadA disciplined SRH bowling unit fought back with a lot of pride as SunRisers Hyderabad beat Chennai Super Kings in a nervy game on Saturday. Defending 194, SRH fought till the end and restricted CSK to 184/8 in 20 overs.

After Neeraj, Sumit harassment complaint, Paralympic committee sacks Naval SinghDronacharya awardee coach Naval Singh has been removed by the Paralympic Committee of India after harassment allegations by Neeraj Chopra and Paralympic gold medallist Sumit Antil.

Ex-Wimbledon champion faces 4-year ban after refusing doping testFormer Wimbledon champion Marketa Vondrousova has been charged by the ITIA over an alleged doping-control breach after an out-of-competition test in December. She denied any wrongdoing, saying the incident was driven by fear and anxiety amid mental health struggles.

RCB blame tacky Chinnaswamy pitch for loss, but team won't overanalyse resultRoyal Challengers Bengaluru suffered their first loss at home in the IPL 2026 season. Captain Rajat Patidar and director of cricket Mo Bobat pinned the loss on the tacky pitch at the M Chinnaswamy Stadium.

Matheesha Pathirana all set to join KKR. Will he play against Rajasthan Royals?IPL 2026: Matheesha Pathirana is set to join Kolkata Knight Riders after receiving post-injury clearance, but it remains to be seen if he will feature immediately, with his fitness to be assessed ahead of their clash with Rajasthan Royals.

Bhuvneshwar Kumar for India again? RCB pacer's heroics vs DC fuels comeback talksIPL 2026, RCB vs DC Ex-India batter Mohammad Kaif hailed Bhuvneshwar Kumar after his vintage spell against Delhi Capitals, saying the 36-year-old still has what it takes to return to the national side.

Emotional David Miller vanquishes last over demons to complete DC's RCB heistDavid Miller vanquished the ghosts of DC's heartbreaking loss against GT to power his side to a famous win at the Chinnaswamy stadium against RCB.

RCB v DC: Stubbs and Miller ensure KL Rahul's birthday bash is on in BengaluruIPL 2026, RCB vs DC: Delhi Capitals chased down 176 on a sluggish Bengaluru pitch on Saturday. Birthday boy KL Rahul struck a fifty before Tristan Stubbs and David Miller produced a clinical finishing act to deny Royal Challengers Bengaluru a climb to the top of the points table.

Chelsea vs Manchester United: High stakes clash as both sides look to bounce backManchester United and Chelsea meet in a high-stakes clash, with both sides looking to recover after disappointing losses last weekend. United face defensive concerns, while Chelsea are under pressure after a poor run of form and mounting scrutiny on their manager.

Why Neeraj hasn't picked up a javelin in last 6 months? Indian star revealsSpeaking to India Today's sister channel, Sports Today, Neeraj Chopra recalled why he split with Jan Zelezny after the eighth-place finish at Tokyo World Championships. Having not thrown a javelin since, the two-time Olympic medalist is preparing for the new season with caution and clarity.

education.headline: The role of data analytics in modern advertising educationThis transformation also exercises a significant impact on the realm of 'advertising education.'

NCERT deletes Azad Pak, adds China's aggression reference in Class 12 textbookThe NCERT has made final changes to its Class 12 Political Science textbook syllabus for the upcoming academic year, including a revision in reference to India's border situation with China, Azad Pakistan, Article 370 and more.

NEET row: Bihar cops find 6 post-dated cheques 'issued for question paper brokers'Six post-dated cheques which were suspected to have been issued in favour of mafia demanding over Rs 30 lakh from each candidate seeking alleged leaked question paper ahead of NEET held last month were recovered by Bihar Police.

Forest fires surge tenfold from 2004 to 2017: Causes, impact and preventionAmidst a relentless surge, intense forest fires are now multiplying tenfold, casting a menacing shadow over our flora and fauna. Year by year, their fiery grip tightens, threatening ecosystems far and wide, now extending their reach to impact human lives.

Fresh plea in Supreme Court to recall NEET results, reconduct examsA fresh petition has been filed in the Supreme Court to recall the National Entrance cum Eligibility Test - Under Graduate (NEET-UG) 2024 results and re-conduct fresh examination after discrepancies in the declared results.

NTA issues clarification after NEET result controversy over toppers, high cut-offIn a detailed statement, the National Testing Agency (NTA) said that the increase in cut-off and number of toppers in the NEET-UG reflect the competitive nature of the exam and maintained that the test's integrity has not been compromised.

Exclusive: Arvind Kejriwal claims 'BJP succession war, PM backing Amit Shah'Speaking exclusively to India Today TV, Delhi Chief Minister Arvind Kejriwal claimed that there is a "succession war" within the BJP. He also claimed that the ruling party is "not fighting the Lok Sabha elections as a team".

4 medical students arrested in Bihar for appearing as proxies for NEET aspirantsFour MBBS students who appeared in place of NEET aspirants in the medical entrance exam were detained in Bihar's Purnea.

NEET-UG question paper not leaked, claims baseless: National Testing AgencyThe reports claiming question paper leak in the medical entrance exam NEET-UG are "completely baseless and without any ground", the National Testing Agency (NTA) has clarified.
The generation with answers: Tech-savvy Indian youth tackling global challengesIndia's tech-savvy youth rewriting the global narrative, one solution at a time.

Left sweeps JNU students' polls, Dhananjay is first Dalit presidentThe Left swept the Jawaharlal Nehru University Students' Union (JNUSU) polls, winning all four posts and trouncing the RSS-affiliated ABVP. The ABVP was leading at one point, but the Left eventually surged ahead and emerged victorious.
The power of knowledge: Education's influence on real estate market trendsIn a dynamic sector like real estate, investing in continuing education is not simply an option; it is a requirement.

JNU students' body election on March 22 after 4 yearsThe Jawaharlal Nehru University Students' Union (JNUSU) polls will take place on March 22, more than four years after the last elections were held in September 2019.

UP 12th board exam math, biology papers leaked on WhatsApp, complaint filedThe mathematics and biology question papers for the Uttar Pradesh class 12 board exam were allegedly leaked on a WhatsApp group.

UP Police Constable recruitment exam cancelled days after paper leakThe Uttar Pradesh Police Constable recruitment exam held on February 17 and 18 was canceled due to a paper leak. The Chief Minister has announced a re-examination within the next six months.
